2604.07102.title: The Impact of Steering Large Language Models with Persona Vectors in Educational Applications
2604.07102.paper_summary: Activation-based steering can personalize large language models at inference time, but its effects in educational settings remain unclear. We study persona vectors for seven character traits in short-answer generation and automated scoring on the ASAP-SAS benchmark across three models spanning two architectures. Persona steering lowers answer quality overall, with much larger effects on open-ended English Language Arts (ELA) prompts than on factual science prompts; interpretive and argumentative tasks are up to 11x more sensitive. On the scoring side, we observe predictable valence-aligned calibration shifts: evil and impolite scorers grade more harshly, while good and optimistic scorers grade more leniently. ELA tasks are 2.5-3x more susceptible to scorer personalization than science tasks, and the Mixture-of-Experts model shows roughly 6x larger calibration shifts than the dense models. To our knowledge, this is the first study to systematically examine the effects of activation-steered persona traits in educational generation and scoring, and the results highlight the need for task-aware and architecture-aware calibration when deploying steered models in educational settings.
2604.07102.paper_summary_zh: 啟動基礎的引導可以在推理時個性化大型語言模型，但其在教育環境中的影響仍不明確。<lb/>我們研究了七種性格特徵的個性向量，在ASAP-SAS基準上進行短答案生成和自動評分，涵蓋了兩種架構的三個模型。<lb/>個性引導整體上降低了答案質量，對開放式英語語言藝術（ELA）提示的影響遠大於對事實科學提示的影響；詮釋性和論證性任務的敏感度高達11倍。<lb/>在評分方面，我們觀察到可預測的情感對齊校準變化：邪惡和不禮貌的評分者評分更嚴格，而善良和樂觀的評分者則評分更寬鬆。<lb/>ELA任務對評分者個性化的敏感度是科學任務的2.5-3倍，而混合專家模型的校準變化約比密集模型大6倍。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一項系統性研究啟動引導的個性特徵在教育生成和評分中的影響的研究，結果突顯了在教育環境中部署引導模型時對任務意識和架構意識的校準需求。
2604.07102.author: Yongchao Wu et.al.
